what_it_does: This file is a structured extraction and interpretation of a virtual-tour product design system across four audience verticals, turning observed slide layouts into a rule-based catalog of visual recipes, narrative grammar, reusable components, attention-flow descriptions, and content-slot samples.
observations: It treats 11 slides as one design language expressed at three compression depths: 4-page architects, 3-page gatehouse, and 2-page property/residential.
It records concrete visual recipes with approximate colors, dimensions, opacities, shadows, and border radii, each paired with a generator rule.
It separates findings into categories such as gradient-shading-opacity, geometry, type-hierarchy, padding-alignment, color-palette, atom, zone, sequence, and attention-direction.
It flags some patterns as emergent and others as non-emergent without explaining the classification criteria.
It documents a single amber/goldenrod accent color carrying all positive, active, aspirational, and attention-routing semantics.
It describes per-page attention flow for every slide, including scan order and focal elements.
It defines nine reusable components with parameters, such as ChevronProcessBar, SplitHeroBlock, StageSection, and ClosingCTAZone.
It includes content samples with character, word, and line budgets for common copy slots.
It notes that the property and residential decks are observed-identical templates.
standouts: The amber accent is the sole action/positive/aspiration signal across the entire system.
The chevron process bar acts as an orientation spine marking live journey stages.
A strict 50/50 left-text/right-photo split is invariant across nearly all pages.
The same narrative grammar scales from 4-page to 2-page decks by compressing or stubbing stages.
conventions: Uses a flat JSON object with descriptive, plain-language keys.
Every finding includes a category, name, recipe, evidence, emergent flag, and generator rule.
Color values are given as approximate hex or rgba ranges rather than exact tokens.
Content samples are annotated with character, word, and line counts.
Reusable components are named in PascalCase and include a reuse note with parameter hints.
It follows a design-substrate ledger convention rather than a code-symbol extraction format.
concerns: Color and spacing values are approximate, which could lead to inconsistent reproduction.
The emergent/non-emergent distinction is asserted without visible criteria.
Some category labels are broad, causing unrelated patterns to sit under one heading.
Generator rules mix visual styling with content constraints, making them brittle.
No source media references, bounding boxes, or extraction methodology are provided.
Property and residential are called identical but are still documented separately, risking future drift.
notes: A design-system autopsy of the ConceptV virtual-tour pitch decks, encoding layout, color, type, and narrative rules as reproducible generator recipes.
questions: What source format and extraction method produced these observations?
How were the approximate color and dimension values measured?
What criteria distinguish emergent from non-emergent patterns?
Who is the intended consumer of these generator rules: designers, developers, or an automated layout system?
Are the content samples transcribed verbatim or reconstructed from memory?
Should the observed-identical property and residential templates be merged into one entry?
How does this file relate to other extracted files in counterpart-design/review/ingest/_extracted?
purpose_vs_actual: n/a — no explicit purpose field is present; the file functions as a design-system extraction and generator rule catalog.
intention_signals: It is trying to convert a set of marketing decks into a deterministic, reusable design system so that future decks or generated layouts can replicate the same visual language, narrative flow, and audience-specific persuasion mechanics without rebuilding each page from scratch.
novelty: It treats pitch-deck pages as a compression grammar with invariant rules across 4/3/2-page depths, and assigns a single accent color the full semantic load of action, positivity, aspiration, and attention routing — a design-token approach applied to static marketing material.
contribution: It contributes a detailed, rule-based inventory of layout, color, typography, component, and content-budget patterns extracted from the virtual-tour product decks, including generator rules, reusable component definitions, and audience-variant grammars that a fused system could reuse when reproducing or extending this design language.
summary_for_embedding: A structured extraction of the ConceptV virtual-tour product pitch-deck design system, documenting shared layout grammar, narrative arc, visual recipes, reusable components, content samples, and generator rules across four audience verticals and three page-depth compressions.
intention_for_embedding: To capture the visual and narrative design language of the virtual-tour product decks as a reproducible, audience-aware system for generating consistent layouts and content.
